key_insight: A microscopic constitutive model links nonaffine elasticity and structural relaxation to explain stress overshoot, yielding, steady flow, and strain hardening in amorphous solids.
engineering_relevance: The framework provides a physically grounded, low-parameter way to predict full stress–strain behavior of metallic and polymer glasses across strain rates.
method: Combine nonaffine elastic response with irreversible many-body relaxation.
Use stretched-exponential relaxation as the main dynamical input.
Include finite chain extensibility to capture polymer strain hardening.
evidence_signal: The model quantitatively reproduces experimental stress–strain curves for metallic glasses, polycarbonate, PMMA, and epoxy resins over a broad range of strain rates.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on reliable relaxation parameters and may require material-specific calibration, especially for complex glassy systems.
paper_title: Microscopic constitutive theory of stress overshoot, yielding, and strain hardening in amorphous materials
